title: STILLE CARBONYLATIVE CROSS-COUPLING
summary: Overview page for the Stille carbonylative cross-coupling describing Pd-catalyzed carbon monoxide insertion into organostannane/electrophile couplings to form ketones with broad chemo- and stereocontrol.
notes: Key concepts: carbon monoxide insertion between oxidative addition and transmetalation manifolds, use of organostannanes with acyl or aryl/vinyl electrophiles, and access to ketones under palladium catalysis.

title: STILLE CARBONYLATIVE CROSS-COUPLING
summary: Application page for the Stille carbonylative cross-coupling featuring (-)-strychnine, C-glycosides, photoactivatable 4-benzoyl-L-phenylalanines, and 5-carbonyluridine analogues, highlighting carbonylative ketone formation in alkaloid, carbohydrate, peptide, and nucleoside synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize CO insertion under Pd catalysis, C-glycoside assembly from stannylglucals and iodoglucals, peptide benzophenone installation, and nucleoside carbonylation chemistry.

title: STILLE CROSS-COUPLING (MIGITA-KOSUGI-STILLE COUPLING)
summary: Overview page for the Stille cross-coupling describing Pd-catalyzed C-C bond formation between organostannanes and aryl, vinyl, allyl, or acyl electrophiles under mild conditions with high functional-group tolerance.
notes: Key concepts: oxidative addition, transmetalation from organostannanes, reductive elimination, compatibility with many functional groups, and frequent preservation of alkene geometry in the coupled product.

title: STILLE CROSS-COUPLING (MIGITA-KOSUGI-STILLE COUPLING)
summary: Application page for the Stille cross-coupling featuring (+)-mycotrienol, ircinal A, quadrigemine C, and callipeltoside aglycon, illustrating vinyl, dienyl, and highly convergent fragment couplings in complex natural-product synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize macrocyclization-compatible Stille coupling, tandem Stille/Diels-Alder and double Stille-Heck strategies, and careful catalyst selection for diene-yne fragment assembly.

title: STILLE-KELLY COUPLING
summary: Overview page for the Stille-Kelly coupling describing Pd-catalyzed intramolecular biaryl formation from aryl halides or triflates and distannanes to furnish arylstannane intermediates and cyclized biaryls.
notes: Key concepts: tandem oxidative addition/transmetalation sequences with distannanes, arylstannane intermediates, intramolecular biaryl formation, and usefulness for medium-ring and macrocyclic biaryl synthesis.

title: STILLE-KELLY COUPLING
summary: Application page for the Stille-Kelly coupling featuring N-mesyl-α-carboline, benzo[4,5]furo[3,2-c]pyridine, hippadine, and plagiochin D, showing intramolecular biaryl couplings in heteroaromatic alkaloids and macrocycles.
notes: Examples emphasize sequential Buchwald-Hartwig/Stille-Kelly planning, heterobiaryl cyclization, indole alkaloid assembly, and macrocyclic biaryl closure from stannylated intermediates.

title: STOBBE CONDENSATION
summary: Overview page for the Stobbe condensation describing base-mediated condensation of aldehydes or ketones with dialkyl succinates to give alkylidene succinic acid monoesters or related half-esters after acidic workup.
notes: Key concepts: succinate enolate addition to carbonyl compounds, aldol-type intermediates, lactone opening and double-bond formation, and preference for monoester/half-acid products after acidification.

title: STOBBE CONDENSATION
summary: Application page for the Stobbe condensation featuring (+)-codeine, a conformationally constrained analogue of Δ8-THC, and 3,5-bis-trifluoromethylbenzylidene diphenylmethylene succinic anhydride, illustrating alkylidene succinate installation in alkaloid, cannabinoid, and stereochemical studies.
notes: Examples emphasize intramolecular carbonyl additions in alkaloid synthesis, in situ hydrogenation/cyclization of diacid intermediates, and definitive determination of E-olefin geometry in highly substituted Stobbe adducts.

title: STORK ENAMINE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Stork enamine synthesis describing condensation of aldehydes or ketones with secondary amines to form enamines that undergo α-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, or related electrophilic functionalization before hydrolysis.
notes: Key concepts: enamine formation from carbonyl compounds and secondary amines, neutral enamine alkylation/acylation, hydrolysis back to α-functionalized carbonyl compounds, and use in Michael-type annulations.

title: STORK ENAMINE SYNTHESIS
summary: Application page for the Stork enamine synthesis featuring (+)-parviflorine, (±)-biactractylolide, (+)-jatropholone A, and (-)-8-aza-12-oxo-17-desoxoestrone, highlighting enamine-mediated alkylation and conjugate addition in terpenoid and alkaloid synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize enamine generation with pyrrolidine or morpholine, use of crude enamines in complex synthesis, acylation to 1,3-diketones, and intramolecular variants for aza-steroid construction.
